expectations.text: Report file is saved as a markdown file at the specified output path
Report contains a Recommendation section with explicit BUY, SELL, or HOLD action
Report contains Conviction level (High, Medium, or Low)
Report contains a Time Horizon for the recommendation
Report has an Executive Summary section
Report has a Quality Metrics Dashboard with Piotroski F-Score, Altman Z-Score, ROIC, and FCF Yield
expectations.evidence: Lines 35-42: Dashboard table with F-Score 6/9, Z-Score 9.40, ROIC 28.79%, FCF Yield 2.55%

expectations.text: Report has a Valuation Summary section with P/E, EV/EBITDA, or P/S multiples
expectations.evidence: Lines 46-52: Valuation table with P/E (FWD) 23.33x, EV/EBITDA 17.54x, P/S 9.95x with historical and peer comparison

expectations.text: Report includes an intrinsic value estimate or margin of safety calculation
expectations.evidence: Lines 54-56: 'Intrinsic Value Estimate: $475 (Method: Forward multiple; 27x on implied forward EPS ~$17.6)', 'Margin of Safety: 13.8%'

expectations.text: Report has a Scenario Analysis with bull, base, and bear cases
Report has an Options Analysis section
expectations.evidence: Lines 87-98: Options Analysis with IV Rank 38%, IV Percentile 80%, put/call ratio 0.72, specific strategy recommendation

expectations.text: Report contains actual current MSFT price data (not fabricated)
expectations.evidence: Line 3: 'Current Price: $409.41' — consistent with real MSFT trading data on 2026-03-10

expectations.text: Report has a Sources section with references
expectations.evidence: Lines 144-158: 13 sources with hyperlinks to Microsoft IR, MarketWatch, StockAnalysis, Macrotrends, Barchart, AlphaQuery

expectations.text: Report includes comparison to market benchmarks (S&P 500 or Nasdaq)
expectations.evidence: Line 4: 'S&P 500 +0.83%, Nasdaq +1.38%, XLK +1.80%' plus peer comparison table

claims.claim: MSFT FY26 Q2 revenue was $81.3B (+17% YoY)
claims.evidence: Consistent with Microsoft investor relations data
Consistent across both with_skill and old_skill outputs

suggestions.assertion: Report has an Options Analysis section
suggestions.reason: A section header with 'N/A' for all fields would still pass. Consider asserting that IV Rank or IV Percentile has a numeric value.
No assertion checks whether the recommendation rationale logically follows from the analysis — both BUY and HOLD could pass with identical data.
No assertion checks the upside/downside ratio calculation or whether the scenario analysis probabilities sum to ~100%.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions verify structural completeness well but don't test analytical quality. The improved skill's main advantage (more complete options data, specific strategy recommendations) goes unmeasured.
